description: Solve fixed-topology cloth or volumetric deformable solids outside Unreal Engine, preserve vertex/tetrahedral state as canonical truth, and replay the derived surface cache in Unreal for sensor capture.
physical_assumptions: The first implementation is a fixed-topology mass-spring cloth without tearing or self-collision.
Gravity and rigid collider response are solver-owned after frame zero.
Pinned constraints and aerodynamic wind response are solver-owned when declared by the CaseSpec.
Volumetric elastic impact uses Genesis FEM with tetrahedral SAP floor contact; the surface mesh is derived from solver vertices.
The canonical vertex cache remains truth; OBJ or Unreal Geometry Cache assets are render proxies.
repair_suggestions: Increase solver substeps before lowering the physical stiffness.
When a high-wind endpoint exceeds the structural stretch hard gate, lower that endpoint instead of relaxing the gate.
Increase collision thickness or mesh resolution when face chords intersect a curved collider.
Reject a UE replay when its frame index or coordinate conversion differs from the canonical cache.
